title: MAY DECEMBER
logline: An actress travels to Georgia to research a woman at the center of a tabloid scandal decades earlier, blurring the lines between performance and reality as she prepares for her role.
antagonistOrConflict: The moral ambiguity and psychological complexity of portraying Gracie Atherton-Yoo, a woman involved in a controversial relationship that became a national scandal

actBreaks.description: Elizabeth finishes her first substantial dinner with the Atherton-Yoo family, having established her role as researcher and begun her psychological infiltration.

validation.ruling: Scene #23 shows the family dinner where Gracie forces Charlie to drink milk for his 'calcium deficiency' while revealing she hunts and kills her own food, demonstrating her controlling nature and capacity for violence. This fundamentally shifts the story from Elizabeth's research visit to her recognition of Gracie's true threatening nature.

actBreaks.description: After the confrontational restaurant dinner and Georgie's revelations outside, Elizabeth and Joe drive back together, marking the end of her observational phase and beginning her active manipulation.

validation.ruling: Scene #70 shows Georgie revealing to Elizabeth the alleged childhood sexual abuse by Gracie's brothers, providing Elizabeth with the darkest possible explanation for Gracie's behavior. This irreversibly changes Elizabeth's understanding and sets up the final confrontation, representing the point of no return in her investigation.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a slow-burn psychological study that masquerades as research methodology. The three-act structure mirrors Elizabeth's process: observation (Act 1), integration (Act 2), and transformation/revelation (Act 3). The pacing is deliberately methodical, reflecting both Elizabeth's methodical approach to her craft and the gradual revelation of moral complexities. Rather than traditional plot beats, the structure follows emotional and psychological shifts, with scenes building layers of discomfort and complicity. The final act becomes increasingly surreal, culminating in Elizabeth's complete absorption of Gracie's identity, suggesting that the line between actress and subject has been entirely erased.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script maintains a consistently deliberate pace with scenes averaging 1.4 pages, creating an almost documentary-like rhythm. Key pacing shifts occur around scene 41 (the crab shack interview) where external perspectives are introduced, and again around scene 64 (the roof conversation) where intimate family dynamics are revealed. The final act accelerates slightly with shorter, more fragmented scenes as Elizabeth's transformation intensifies.

incitingIncident.description: Gracie opens the package at the garbage, revealing it contains excrement, dismissing it as routine harassment from 'some sicko.'

validation.ruling: Scene #11 is just Elizabeth's arrival at the party, which doesn't shatter her ordinary world. Scene #12 shows Gracie opening the package to reveal excrement - this is the first glimpse of the darkness beneath Gracie's perfect facade, establishing the true conflict Elizabeth will investigate and forcing her to confront that her subject isn't what she expected.

midpoint.description: Elizabeth meets with Morris Sperber, Gracie's former defense attorney, who describes defending her case and explains how Gracie maintained her innocence by claiming Joe seduced her.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. CRAB SHACK RESTAURANT - TYBEE ISLAND-- DAY

validation.ruling: Scene #41 at the Crab Shack shows Elizabeth meeting Morris, Gracie's defense attorney, who reveals crucial backstory about defending Gracie and ending up on the front page of the New York Times. This shifts Elizabeth from passive observer to active investigator seeking the truth about Gracie's case, marking her transition from reactive to proactive.

climax.description: Gracie arrives confidently at the graduation in white despite whispers from the crowd, meeting Elizabeth who also wears white and now carries herself as Gracie's equal.
climax.sceneHeading: EXT. FOOTBALL FIELD-- A BIT LATER

validation.ruling: Scene #86 presents the final confrontation between Elizabeth and Gracie at the graduation, where Elizabeth has transformed and now stands as Gracie's equal. Gracie reveals she knows about Georgie's revelations and makes her final manipulative move, demonstrating Elizabeth's character arc completion as she now truly understands who Gracie is.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE OUTDOORS-- DAWN
scenes.summary: A monarch butterfly lands on a milkweed plant, lays a white egg, and flies away, revealing hundreds of similar eggs on leaves.

scenes.heading: EXT. HISTORIC DISTRICT - SAVANNAH, GEORGIA-- DAY
scenes.summary: Memorial Day morning in historic Savannah with American flags and a high school marching band assembling near a park.

scenes.heading: EXT. INN - HISTORIC SAVANNAH-- DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth Berry pulls her luggage from a rental car in front of a Victorian inn.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth unpacks her inn room while on a phone call, discovers a gift basket with a note from management praising her TV show.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Elizabeth sits on the bed, opens a new notebook, writes 'Gracie' and underlines it while thinking.

scenes.heading: EXT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE - BACKYARD - TYBEE ISLAND - DAY
scenes.summary: Joe Yoo tends to a grill in the lush backyard of the Atherton-Yoo house on Tybee Island.

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE - KITCHEN-- DAY
scenes.summary: Gracie prepares cakes in the kitchen with friend Rhonda while Joe gets his second beer, prompting Gracie to comment on his drinking.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe grills hot dogs at the barbecue while friend Ben admits to looking up Elizabeth Berry online, their conversation interrupted by a child's scream.

scenes.heading: EXT. CATALINA DRIVE--DAY
scenes.summary: Point-of-view shot through a windshield driving past moss-draped oaks and residential homes.

scenes.heading: EXT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE-- DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth arrives at the Atherton-Yoo house, picks up a brown package from the front step when no one answers, and heads toward backyard sounds.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Elizabeth enters the Memorial Day barbecue and meets Gracie for the first time, with Gracie immediately taking the package from her.
scenes.turningPointNote: First meeting between Elizabeth and Gracie, establishing their central relationship

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gracie opens the package at the garbage, revealing it contains excrement, dismissing it as routine harassment from 'some sicko.'

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Elizabeth wanders the party writing in her notebook, watching Gracie and meeting Mary and her teenage friends who are curious about her TV career.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD-- LATER AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: At the settled afternoon barbecue, Elizabeth observes Gracie cuddled with Joe while talking to family friend Rhonda about the couple.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elizabeth reads a 1992 People Magazine about Gracie's scandal featuring headlines about her relationship with 13-year-old Joe and their child.

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO DEN-- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe sits at the family computer watching videos and clips of Elizabeth from talk shows, award ceremonies, and commercials.

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO BEDROOM-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe gets into bed smelling like smoke, leading to an argument with Gracie about showering before bed that ends with her crying and him apologizing.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD-- EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe carefully searches milkweed bushes at dawn, delicately clipping leaves with monarch butterfly eggs onto a tray.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe places the egg-bearing leaves into white mesh cages in the living room and texts a photo to a monarch butterfly Facebook group.

scenes.heading: EXT. COMMUNITY CENTER-- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Elizabeth gets out of her car and approaches a community center building.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMUNITY CENTER-- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Elizabeth approaches a flower-arranging class through glass doors where Gracie spots her and waves.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMUNITY CENTER-- LATER
scenes.summary: Elizabeth and Gracie share a table making flower arrangements, with Gracie giving advice about order and taking over Elizabeth's more natural design while asking about her engagement ring.

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO KITCHEN-- DAY
scenes.summary: At dinner, Gracie serves quail and forces Charlie to drink milk for his calcium deficiency while Elizabeth tries to make conversation, with Joe remaining passive during the uncomfortable meal.

scenes.heading: EXT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE-- DUSK
scenes.summary: Elizabeth asks Joe if she can come to work with him, then realizes they're the same age and comments on their different life stages regarding children.

scenes.heading: INT. INN - BATHROOM-- MORNING
scenes.summary: Elizabeth puts on makeup while talking to her fiancé Aaron on speaker phone about Gracie's apparent lack of guilt or shame.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHIPPEWA SQUARE - HISTORIC SAVANNAH - DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth walks quickly through Chippewa Square past a historical tour group, checking a note for her destination.

scenes.heading: INT. GALLERY ESPRESSO - DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth meets Tom Atherton at Gallery Espresso where he's ordered her coffee and expresses willingness to talk about the past scandal.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elizabeth begins interviewing key figures from Gracie's past, expanding her investigation

scenes.heading: INT. THE POSH LOFT - HISTORIC SAVANNAH-- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: At a dress shop, Gracie helps Mary try on a white graduation dress while Elizabeth watches, and Gracie mentions hearing about Elizabeth's meeting with Tom.

scenes.heading: EXT. PET STORE-- DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth takes a photo of the exterior of Telfair Pets, the former pet store.

scenes.heading: INT. PET STORE-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Pet store owner Mr. Henderson gives Elizabeth a tour, explaining how his mother hired Gracie as an employee after business declined due to K-Mart competition.

scenes.heading: INT. STOCKROOM-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Elizabeth enters the pet store stockroom alone, walking through the grey space with shelves and aisles where the scandal occurred.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK OF THE STOCKROOM-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: In the back of the stockroom, Elizabeth explores the private area with wooden stairs, lies down on them, and physically reenacts what might have happened there, covering her own mouth.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elizabeth's disturbing physical reenactment of the abuse scene represents a psychological breaking point in her research

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM-- DAY
scenes.summary: The monarch butterfly eggs in Joe's living room cages have hatched into thick green caterpillars that writhe and eat constantly.

scenes.heading: EXT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE - POOL-- DAY
scenes.summary: Three workers resume construction on the Atherton-Yoo family pool, visible through the living room window.

scenes.heading: INT. DEN-- DAY
scenes.summary: Joe looks through old family photos and discovers a pink envelope with handwritten pages hidden in a Karate Kid photo book, reads some lines, then quickly puts it away.

scenes.heading: INT. DEN-- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Joe texts with Michela from the monarch Facebook group about her bad day, hesitates over responding to her mention of taking a bath, but stops when Gracie enters.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN-- MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Joe helps Gracie reach sprinkles from a high cabinet while she makes layer cakes and asks about graduation plans and his butterfly hobby.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elizabeth examines a photo of Gracie holding newborn Honor, discovering Gracie's ankle was handcuffed to the hospital bed when she unfolds the bottom of the picture.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Elizabeth uses her inhaler then watches audition videos of 13-year-old boy actors auditioning to play young Joe, disturbed by how young they look.

scenes.heading: INT. X-RAY ROOM-- DAY
scenes.summary: In the X-ray room, Joe shows Elizabeth slides of a 13-year-old's broken arm from skateboarding, and Elizabeth comments on how small the child's hand looks compared to hers.

scenes.heading: INT. CRAB SHACK RESTAURANT - TYBEE ISLAND-- DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth meets with Morris Sperber, Gracie's former defense attorney, who describes defending her case and explains how Gracie maintained her innocence by claiming Joe seduced her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Morris reveals Gracie's defense strategy of claiming the child seduced her, a key revelation about her mindset

scenes.heading: EXT. JOE SR.’S APARTMENT-- DAY
scenes.summary: Joe walks across the lawn carrying groceries to his father's apartment building.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE SR.’S APARTMENT-- DAY
scenes.summary: Joe enters his father's modest apartment with his own key and helps Joe Sr. open a stuck glass door.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOE SR.’S BALCONY-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe and his father sit on the balcony discussing graduation plans and the approaching empty nest, with Joe Sr. smoking heavily and suggesting Joe will have time for other things.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL-- DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth walks in slow motion through a high school hallway between classes as young male students stare at her, with one freshman boy jumping to impress her.

scenes.heading: INT. BLACK BOX THEATER-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: In the high school theater, Elizabeth answers student Cameron's question about acting in sex scenes, explaining how they can be mechanical or emotionally affecting.

scenes.heading: EXT. ELIZABETH’S CAR-- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: High wide angle shot of Elizabeth's car driving toward the Atherton-Yoo house.

scenes.heading: EXT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE-- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Elizabeth gives Mary a ride home, with Mary appearing unwell and quickly slamming into the house while Elizabeth follows and rings the bell.

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO BATHROOM-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Gracie and Elizabeth sit in the bathroom where Gracie applies makeup to Elizabeth's face while Elizabeth takes notes on the products used.

scenes.heading: INT. DEN-- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Joe watches 'This Old House' on PBS and becomes hypnotized by the sexual connotations of a woman caulking bathroom tile before Gracie interrupts with 'knock-knock.'

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Elizabeth, wearing Gracie's makeup, watches Joe tend to his caterpillars with subtle seductive energy, asking how long he's been raising monarchs.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEIGHBORHOOD-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe and Elizabeth walk alone through the neighborhood as Joe expresses concern about how she'll represent their story, feeling people see him as a victim.

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE-- DUSK
scenes.summary: Joe returns home with takeout to find the house dark and silent, immediately sensing something is wrong.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe finds Gracie silently weeping on the bed after losing a cake order when a client cancelled due to their presence.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN-- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gracie presents Joe with a large three-layer coconut cake and watches expectantly as he eats and tells her it's good.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- MORNING
scenes.summary: Elizabeth talks to director Roberto on the phone about extending her research stay while browsing the Sex Offender Registry online.

scenes.heading: EXT. AIRPORT-- DAY
scenes.summary: Honor arrives at the airport and reunites with Joe and Mary, who help load her suitcases into the car.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe's car winds along the highway from Savannah toward Tybee as they discuss Honor's roommate situation.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CAR-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: In the car, Honor asks about their mother and the actress, causing Mary to roll her eyes mockingly which makes the girls laugh.

scenes.heading: INT. INN - TV SCREEN-- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Elizabeth watches a bad TV movie adaptation of Gracie's story featuring an overly composed, Mrs. Robinson-like portrayal of Gracie.

scenes.heading: INT. DEN-- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Joe texts with Michela from his den while the TV movie plays, discussing their mutual exhaustion and work troubles.

scenes.heading: INT. DEN-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe suggests a vacation to Mexico's butterfly sanctuary to Michela, then apologizes when she reminds him he's married, hitting his forehead in remorse.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Elizabeth applies Gracie's makeup technique while the TV movie's seduction scene plays in the background.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOF-- LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Joe lies on the roof lost in thought until Charlie joins him, discussing college and Charlie's eagerness to leave home.

scenes.heading: INT. FRONT HALL-- LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Elizabeth arrives early for dinner wearing Gracie's makeup style, and Gracie throws her an apron somewhat forcefully to help cook.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN-- A FEW MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Gracie teaches Elizabeth to make caramel by watching bubbling sugar carefully, then instructs her to add butter and cream while stirring furiously.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE ROOF-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe nearly falls off the roof when Gracie and Elizabeth appear below in matching aprons, then starts laughing uncontrollably as they call out in concern.

scenes.heading: INT. GARIBALDI’S - HISTORIC SAVANNAH-- EARLY EVENING
scenes.summary: The family dines at Garibaldi's restaurant where Gracie makes a toast celebrating the twins' graduation and their next phase together, while tension simmers beneath the surface.

scenes.heading: INT. GARIBALDI’S - LADIES’ ROOM-- LATER
scenes.summary: Gracie confides to Elizabeth in the restaurant bathroom about keeping low expectations and her disappointment that tonight didn't go as hoped.

scenes.heading: EXT. GARIBALDI’S - HISTORIC SAVANNAH-- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elizabeth sits outside the restaurant where Georgie approaches and hints at childhood trauma as his theory about Joe, piquing her interest.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CAR-- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe drives Elizabeth back to the inn in silence after she mentions outstaying her welcome, sharing that his sister also had asthma.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe helps Elizabeth with her nebulizer treatment in her inn room, gives her a saved letter in a pink envelope, and she kisses him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elizabeth crosses the line from observer to participant by kissing Joe, fundamentally altering her relationship with the story she's researching

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO BATHROOM-- LATER
scenes.summary: Joe steps out of the shower at home and stares at himself in the bathroom mirror.

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO BEDROOM-- LATER
scenes.summary: Joe watches Gracie sleep from a chair in their dark bedroom, then tells her he wants to discuss things about their relationship they've never talked about, causing her to tense up.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joe confronts Gracie about their relationship's origins for the first time, breaking years of silence about their foundational trauma

scenes.heading: EXT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Atherton-Yoo house sits dark from the front with a shimmer of black trees.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elizabeth sits facing the mirror channeling Gracie's voice and mannerisms as she reads from what appears to be the letter Joe gave her.

scenes.heading: INT. DEN-- DAWN
scenes.summary: At dawn, a monarch butterfly emerges from its chrysalis in the den while Gracie packs in the background.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Gracie heads into the woods in full hunting gear with a rifle and her spaniels following behind.

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO BEDROOM-- MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe wakes up alone in the bright bedroom, still dressed on top of the blankets, realizing Gracie is gone.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe prepares pancake breakfast for five in the empty kitchen and notices movement in the butterfly cages.

scenes.heading: INT. DEN-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe finds the newly hatched butterfly and gently coaxes it onto his finger before unzipping the cage.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe releases the butterfly through the kitchen window as Mary arrives in her white graduation dress, asking for coffee instead of breakfast.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS-- DAY
scenes.summary: Gracie encounters a rabid red fox in the woods that slinks toward her hissing, and her expression shifts from blankness to overwhelming sympathy and despair.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CAR-- MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe drops off his three children at the high school graduation in their red robes, watching them adoringly as they walk to the entrance.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOOTBALL FIELD-- DAY
scenes.summary: The valedictorian addresses the graduating class in blood-red robes before the principal begins calling names for diploma presentation.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOOTBALL FIELD-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Gracie arrives confidently at the graduation in white despite whispers from the crowd, meeting Elizabeth who also wears white and now carries herself as Gracie's equal.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elizabeth has transformed into Gracie's equal, marking the completion of her psychological absorption of Gracie's identity

scenes.heading: INT. SOUNDSTAGE-- DAY OR NIGHT
scenes.summary: On a film soundstage, Elizabeth as Gracie repeatedly performs a scene with a young actor as Joe, handling a snake while Roberto directs multiple takes.
scenes.turningPointNote: The story shifts to Elizabeth's film production, revealing her ultimate use of the intimate knowledge she gained
